# EPICS Database Builder

{{ code_ci }} {{ docs_ci }} {{ coverage }} {{ pypi_version }} {{ license }}

This Python module is designed to make it easy to build complex EPICS databases
by writing a Python script. While writing record templates the corresponding
dbd entries are checked to reduce the number of errors in the database.

```




PyPI

pip install epicsdbbuilder

Source code

https://github.com/dls-controls/epicsdbbuilder

Documentation

https://dls-controls.github.io/epicsdbbuilder
```

A simple example of the use of this library is the following:

```python
from epicsdbbuilder import *
InitialiseDbd('/dls_sw/epics/R3.14.12.3/base/')
SetTemplateRecordNames()

a = records.ao('TEST')
c = records.calc('CALC', CALC = 'A+B', SCAN = '1 second', INPA = a.VAL)
c.INPB = c

WriteRecords('output.db')
```

% Anything below this line is used when viewing README.rst and will be replaced
% when included in index.rst

See <https://dls-controls.github.io/epicsdbbuilder> for more detailed documentation.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.const_array.ConstArray.FormatDb
documentation: epicsdbbuilder callback

Constant Link Values. EPICS Base 3.16.1 and above.

Example: PY Source
------------------
`r = records.lsi('r', INP=ConstArray(['Plain String not DBLINK']))`

Example: Generated DB
---------------------
`field(INP, ["Plain String not DBLINK"])`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.bits.Extend_mbbiDirect
documentation: Extend the automatically generated mbbiDirect and mbboDirect classes
with bit and register methods so they behave a little like hardware.

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.bits._Bits

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.bits._Bits._makeBit
documentation: This function implements either bi or bo depending on direction.
Factory for building bits from an existing record.  The parent record
(generally assumed to be an mbbi record) should present the bits to be
read or written in fields B0 to BF.
Depending on direction, bi or bo can be invoked to create records linking
to the parent record.  Also bit and register can be used to postpone
the creation of real records!

modules.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordnames
documentation: Support for default record name configurations.

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordnames._RecordNames

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordnames.SimpleRecordNames
documentation: Default record name support: each record is created with precisely the name

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.mydbstatic.dbVerify
documentation: Fallback implementation for dbVerify.  Turns out not to be present in EPICS
3.16, which is rather annoying.  In this case we just allow all writes to
succeed.

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.mydbstatic.ImportFunctions
documentation: This function is called late to complete the process of importing all the
exports from this module.  This is done late so that paths.EPICS_BASE can be
configured late.

modules.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase
documentation: Support for generating epics records.

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.quote_char
documentation: Quotes a single character if necessary

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.quote_string
documentation: Converts a string into a safely quoted string with quotation marks

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.PP
documentation:
"Process Passive": any record update through a PP output link will be
processed if its scan is Passive.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = PP(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other PP")`
Some helper routines for building links

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.CA
documentation:
"Channel Access": a CA (input or output) link will be treated as
a channel access link regardless whether it is a DB link or not.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = CA(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other CA")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.CP
documentation:
"Channel Process": a CP input link will cause the linking record
to process any time the linked record is updated.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = CP(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other CP")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.CPP
documentation:
"Channel Process if Passive": a CP input link will be treated as
a channel access link and if the linking record is passive,
the linking passive record will be processed any time the linked record
is updated.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = CPP(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other CPP")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.MS
documentation:
"Maximise Severity": any alarm state on the linked record is propagated
to the linking record. When propagated, the alarm status will become
`LINK_ALARM`.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = MS(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other MS")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.MSS
documentation:
"Maximise Status and Severity": both alarm status and alarm severity
on the linked record are propagated to the linking record.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = MSS(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other MSS")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.MSI
documentation:
"Maximise Severity if Invalid": propagate an alarm state on the linked
record only if the alarm severity is `INVALID_ALARM`.
When propagated, the alarm status will become `LINK_ALARM`.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = MSI(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other MSI")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.NMS
documentation:
"Non-Maximise Severity": no alarm is propagated.
This is the default behavior of EPICS links.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = NMS(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other NMS")`

functions.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.NP
documentation:
"No Process": the linked record is not processed.
This is the default behavior of EPICS links.

Example (Python source)
-----------------------
`my_record.INP = NP(other_record)`

Example (Generated DB)
----------------------
`field(INP, "other NPP")`

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.CreateSubclass
documentation: Creates a subclass of the record with the given record type and
validator bound to the subclass.  The device used to load the record is
remembered so that it can subsequently be instantiated if necessary.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__init__
documentation: Record constructor.  Needs to be told the type of record that this will
be, a field validation object (which will be used to check field names
and field value assignments), the name of the record being created, and
initialisations for any other fields.  Builds standard record name using
the currently configured RecordName hook.
Record constructor.
This is used to construct a record of a particular record type.  The
record is added to database of the generated IOC, or can simply be
written out to a separate .db file, depending on the chosen IOC writer.
record
The name of the record being generated.  The detailed name of the
record is determined by the configured record name convention, and
normally the device part of the record name is not specified here.
**fields
All of the fields supported by the record type appear as attributes
of the class.  Values can be specified in the constructor, or can be
assigned subsequently to the generated instance.
For example, the following code generates a record which counts how
many times it has been processed:
cntr = records.calc('CNTR', CALC = 'A+1', VAL = 0)
cntr.A = cntr
This will generate a database somewhat like this:
record(calc, "$(DEVICE):CNTR")
field(A,    "$(DEVICE):CNTR")
field(CALC, "A+1")
field(VAL,  "0")
Record links can be wrapped with PP(), CP(), MS() and NP() calls.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.Print
documentation: Call to generate database description of this record.  Outputs record
definition in .db file format.  Hooks for meta-data can go here.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__str__
documentation: The string for a record is just its name.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__repr__
documentation: The representation string for a record identifies its type and name,
but we can't do much more.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__call__
documentation: Calling the record generates a self link with a list of specifiers.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__setattr__
documentation: Assigning to a record attribute updates a field.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__ValidateField
documentation: Field validation

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__FormatFieldForDb
documentation: Field formatting

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__delattr__
documentation: Allow individual fields to be deleted from the record.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__getattr__
documentation: Reading a record attribute returns a link to the field.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.ValidFieldName
documentation: Can be called to validate the given field name, returns True iff this
record type supports the given field name.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.Record.__reduce__
documentation: When a record is pickled for export it will reappear as an ImportRecord
instance.  This makes more sense (as the record has been fully generated
already), and avoids a lot of trouble.
Record class
Base class for all record types.
All record types known to the IOC builder (loaded from DBD files in EPICS
support modules) are subclasses of this class.

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase.ImportRecord
documentation: Records can be imported by name.  An imported record has no specification
of its type, and so no validation can be done: all that can be done to an
imported record is to link to it.

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase._Link

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.recordbase._Link.Value
documentation: Returns the value currently assigned to this field.
A link is a class to encapsulate a process variable link.  It remembers
the record, the linked field, and a list of specifiers (such as PP, CP,
etcetera).

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.parameter.Parameter
documentation: A Parameter is used to wrap a template parameter before being assigned to a
record field.

modules.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd
documentation: Implements the set of records provided by a dbd

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.RecordTypes

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.RecordTypes.__contains__
documentation: Checks whether the given recordType names a known valid record type.
This class contains all the record types current supported by the loaded
dbd, and is published to the world as epics.records.  As records are added
(in response to calls to LoadDbdFile) they are automatically available to
all targets.

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.ValidateDbField

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.ValidateDbField.__ProcessDbd
documentation: Computes list of valid names and creates associated arginfo
definitions.  This is postponed quite late to try and ensure the menus
are fully populated, in other words we don't want to fire this until
all the dbd files have been loaded.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.ValidateDbField.ValidFieldName
documentation: This method raises an attribute error if the given field name is
invalid.

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.ValidateDbField.ValidFieldValue
documentation: This method raises an exeption if the given field name does not exist
or if the value cannot be validly written.
This class uses a the static database to validate whether the associated
record type allows a given value to be written to a given field.

classes.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.DBEntry

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.DBEntry.iterate_records
documentation: Iterate through the record types, yielding their names

methods.name: epicsdbbuilder.dbd.DBEntry.iterate_fields
documentation: Iterate through a record's fields, yielding their names

Create a dbEntry instance within the current DBD.

This is a stateful pointer that can be moved to different
record types and fields within them with the iterate methods.

If entry is specified on init and is a DBEntry instance, it
will be copied so that its position is maintained.
